# neokapi

> neokapi is an open-source, format-aware content engine written in Go. It parses
> localization, document, and data formats into a faithful content model, then
> translates, leverages translation memory, and runs verification checks for
> terminology, QA, and brand voice — for content written by people or AI agents.
> `kapi` is the command-line and desktop application built on the engine.

neokapi reimagines the Okapi Framework in Go. The engine extracts translatable
text from any format into a streaming content model (Block / Run / Overlay),
processes it through a pipeline of composable tools, and writes the original
format back faithfully — structure, markup, and placeholders intact. It runs
offline by default with an embedded SQLite translation memory and termbase, and
exposes its capabilities to AI assistants over MCP.

## Getting started

- [Kapi overview](https://neokapi.github.io/kapi/overview): what kapi is and how it relates to the engine
- [Quickstart](https://neokapi.github.io/kapi/get-started/quickstart): install and run the first commands
- [Installation](https://neokapi.github.io/kapi/get-started/installation): Homebrew, WinGet, binary, desktop app
- [First project](https://neokapi.github.io/kapi/get-started/first-project): the `.kapi` recipe and project model
- [Use with Claude](https://neokapi.github.io/kapi/get-started/use-with-claude): drive the engine from an AI assistant over MCP

## Framework (Go library)

- [Go quickstart](https://neokapi.github.io/framework/go-quickstart): embed the engine in a Go program
- [Architecture](https://neokapi.github.io/framework/architecture): streaming pipeline, content model, executor
- [Formats](https://neokapi.github.io/framework/formats): format-aware readers and writers, plugins, okapi-bridge
- [AI translation](https://neokapi.github.io/framework/ai-translation): LLM and MT backends, composed in the pipeline
- [Tools](https://neokapi.github.io/framework/tools): the built-in processing tools

## Reference

- [Reference overview](https://neokapi.github.io/reference): generated and interactive references
- [CLI commands](https://neokapi.github.io/commands): every kapi command
- [Format reference](https://neokapi.github.io/formats): the full, generated list of supported formats
- [Tool reference](https://neokapi.github.io/tools): the full, generated list of tools
- [Project file](https://neokapi.github.io/reference/project-file): the `.kapi` recipe format
- [MCP server](https://neokapi.github.io/reference/mcp): the engine's MCP tools

## Toolbox and React

- [CLI toolbox](https://neokapi.github.io/toolbox/overview): kgrep, ksed, kcat — format-aware grep/sed/cat
- [Kapi React](https://neokapi.github.io/react/introduction): zero-config i18n library for React

## Source

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/neokapi/neokapi): source, issues, releases (Apache-2.0)
